book: Understanding Society: India and Beyond (Part 1) — New NCERT 2026-27
subject: Social Science
note: Part 2 chapters will be added here when NCERT releases the book.

chapters.name: Atmosphere and Climate
homework: Name the two main gases of the atmosphere along with their approximate percentages. (1 mark)

Assertion (A): The troposphere is the most important layer of the atmosphere for us. Reason (R): Almost all weather phenomena like rain, fog and hailstorm occur in this layer. Choose the correct option: (a) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A. (b) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A. (c) A is true but R is false. (d) A is false but R is true. (1 mark)
Why does the temperature generally decrease as we go higher in the troposphere? (3 marks)
What is the ozone layer? In which layer of the atmosphere is it found, and why is it important for life on Earth? (3 marks)
Differentiate between weather and climate with one example of each. (3 marks)
List any four ways in which you can reduce your carbon footprint in daily life. (3 marks)
Explain the mechanism of the monsoon in India. Why is the monsoon so important for our country? (5 marks)

questions.q: Which gas makes up the largest portion of the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere. Oxygen is second at about 21%.

questions.q: Almost all weather phenomena — rain, fog, hailstorm — occur in which layer of the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: The troposphere is the lowest layer, where water vapour and dust are present, so all weather changes happen here.

questions.q: The ozone layer, which protects us from the Sun's harmful ultraviolet rays, is found in the:
questions.explanation: The ozone layer lies in the stratosphere and absorbs most of the harmful UV radiation coming from the Sun.

questions.q: The monsoon is best described as:
options: The seasonal reversal of wind direction
questions.explanation: Monsoon comes from the Arabic word 'mausim' meaning season — it is the seasonal reversal of winds that brings rain to India.

questions.q: Which of the following actions helps reduce your carbon footprint?
options: Cycling or walking for short distances
questions.explanation: Walking or cycling instead of using vehicles reduces the burning of fuel, which lowers carbon dioxide emissions.
